title: How Making It Rain Leads Work at eXp Realty
excerpt: Making It Rain at eXp Realty runs PPC ads for you, sending buyer and seller leads into your CRM. Smart Agent Alliance shows how to make it pay off.
content:
Key Takeaway: Making It Rain at eXp Realty is a brokerage-operated PPC advertising program that runs Google and Meta ads on behalf of agents and delivers buyer and seller leads directly into the agent’s CRM. It matters most to agents who want paid online leads without personally managing ad platforms, vendors, or campaign setup.

TL;DR About Making It Rain at eXp Realty

  • Making It Rain is eXp Realty’s internal PPC advertising program
  • Campaigns are managed by eXp’s in-house marketing team
  • Ads run on Google and Meta platforms under the agent’s branding
  • Leads are delivered directly into the agent’s connected CRM
  • No referral fee is charged at closing for these leads
  • Participation is optional and billed monthly
  • The program sits inside eXp’s broader Revenos lead ecosystem

Many agents want online buyer and seller leads but do not want to become part-time digital advertisers. Managing Google PPC, Meta ads, targeting rules, compliance, landing pages, and tracking requires time, testing, and ongoing adjustment. Most agents either outsource this work to third-party vendors or stop altogether after inconsistent results.

Making It Rain exists to remove that operational burden by centralizing paid advertising inside the brokerage.

This article explains how Making It Rain at eXp Realty fits into the broader eXp Realty leads ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

Which Brokerages Offer Lead Generation And Why Making It Rain Stands Out
Making It Rain Lead Types: Home Search, Express Offers, FlexReach
How Making It Rain Connects To Your CRM And Tech For Auto Leads
What Does Making It Rain Cost?
Are Making It Rain Leads Any Good? Follow Up, Quality, And Results
What Agents Also Ask About Making It Rain
Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty
Frequently Asked Questions
Related eXp Realty Lead Tools

How Does Making It Rain Work?

Most agents who search for “Which brokerages offer lead generation services for real estate agents” are not looking for slogans. They want a real system. Making It Rain eXp Realty is that system inside eXp. It was created by Glenn Sanford when the company started and now sits inside eXp Revenos, eXp’s full-time lead generation division. In simple terms, Making It Rain eXp Realty is the in house real estate PPC leads program that sits under eXp Revenos and powers Google PPC real estate leads and Facebook ads for real estate agents, all tied directly into eXp’s CRM choices.

You begin by picking your campaign type, budget, and target area. Inside the Making It Rain dashboard you choose Home Search Leads, Express Offers leads, or FlexReach, then set your ad spend. eXp’s internal team handles the tech, targeting, and tracking.

Mary H. talks about eXp’s model as “built for agents,” because the training, collaboration, and systems all work together. She says a big part of her consistency comes from leaning into eXp’s offerings instead of trying to build everything alone. The relationships and tools gave her a more steady, predictable business base.

In plain English, Making It Rain is like hiring a full-time ad team and paying them a gym membership fee instead of a full salary.

Which Brokerages Offer Lead Generation And Why Making It Rain Stands Out

Many brokerages say they “provide leads,” but most rely on third party portals, team leaders, or office floor time. eXp Realty is different. It built a full-time internal lead division, Revenos, and programs like Making It Rain and Zoocasa.

Agents search things like “Best brokerages for agents looking to get buyer and seller leads” or “Brokerages offering paid lead packages for real estate agents” because they feel stuck between expensive portals and broker promises. Traditional firms often depend on Zillow, Realtor.com, or office leads that appear and disappear with the market.

At eXp, Revenos and Making It Rain are built into the model. Instead of waiting for your broker to hand you a lead, you log in, choose your campaign, and let eXp’s marketing team run the ads under your name and branding. You are not fighting over a shared pool. You are launching your own pipeline.

This also answers a quiet question: “How do brokerages provide leads to their affiliated agents?” In many places, it is through random rotation or whoever is in the office. With Making It Rain, the agent who orders the campaign gets the leads. No games, no silent priority list.

Artemisa B. came to the U.S. from Mexico and started from zero in a new market. Over time, by leaning into the systems and support at eXp, she closed 223 deals as a solo agent. She credits the company’s tools and leverage for letting her grow far beyond what her old brokerage model allowed.

If the average “lead program” is a leaky bucket, Making It Rain is closer to a faucet you can actually turn on and off without breaking the handle.

Making It Rain Lead Types: Home Search, Express Offers, FlexReach

Making It Rain offers three main campaign types. Home Search Leads capture buyers searching Google for homes in your area. Express Offers Leads attract motivated sellers who want cash or quick options. FlexReach is a flexible product that promotes events, retargets your database, or reaches custom audiences using uploaded lists or geotargeting.

When agents ask “Brokerages that supply exclusive leads to their agents?” what they really mean is “Can I choose the type of business I want?” Making It Rain is structured around that idea. Each campaign type serves a clear purpose and uses channels that match how buyers and sellers actually behave online.

Home Search Leads

These campaigns focus on buyers searching phrases like “homes for sale in Austin” or “three bedroom house in Novato.” eXp’s team runs Google PPC ads that send people to your IDX home search site. They register to see more listings. The lead lands in your CRM with search behavior attached for smarter follow up.

Express Offers Leads

Express Offers leads are seller focused. These campaigns speak to owners who are open to cash offers, convenience, or speed. The ads often run on Facebook and drive people to a simple form. These are highly motivated sellers, but they still need options, education, and a skilled agent to help them choose the best path.

FlexReach

FlexReach is the flexible tool. You can upload a list of past clients, event registrants, or cold leads, or you can geotarget a neighborhood. eXp runs ads across channels like Google and Meta, and in some campaigns even Amazon, to stay in front of that specific audience. It is perfect for events, open houses, webinars, or “Is now a good time to sell?” reminders.

Together, Home Search Leads, Express Offers leads, and FlexReach form the core of the Making It Rain leads system at eXp Realty, giving agents a clear mix of buyer, seller, and custom campaign options inside one program.

Agent Joel B. used FlexReach to promote an “Agents Helping Agents” event in Buffalo. He uploaded a list of local agents and let the system target them across multiple platforms. The people he wanted to reach saw the event everywhere. Ticket sales jumped, and the audience showed up already warmed up by the marketing.

FlexReach is basically the Swiss Army knife of Making It Rain, only you will actually use this tool more than once.

How Making It Rain Connects To Your CRM And Tech For Auto Leads

Making It Rain leads do not sit in your email. They are pushed straight into your CRM, such as Lofty, or BoldTrail. The MIR dashboard shows clicks, leads, and performance. You adjust locations, budgets, and campaign types in one place, then let your automations handle speed to lead and follow up.

Agents who search “Brokerages with technology platforms that generate leads automatically?” or “Brokerages that integrate CRM systems for lead management?” are looking for exactly this setup. At eXp, Making It Rain runs the ads. Your CRM receives the contacts. You build automation rules once, then let the system notify you like a digital assistant that never sleeps.

Once a Home Search or Express Offers lead registers, your CRM can send a text, email, and task in seconds. You can also tag FlexReach leads differently, such as past clients or “likely seller,” so the right scripts and follow up plans fire.

Inside the MIR dashboard, you see what is happening. You can filter by campaign, see cost per lead, and stop or change campaigns without opening three different ad platforms. For agents who are still juggling spreadsheets and sticky notes, this alone feels like air.

ICON agent Raj J. explains that fifty to sixty percent of his business comes from a mix of sphere referrals and online leads through tools like Boldtrail landing pages. He says eXp’s systems helped him diversify, automate, and keep scaling even when others slowed down, because the tech kept feeding his pipeline.

If your current “CRM integration” is just copying phone numbers from your inbox, Making It Rain will feel like moving from a flip phone to a smart phone overnight.

What Does Making It Rain Cost?

Making It Rain is a paid monthly subscription that starts around $250 per month. You do not pay referral fees at closing. Membership is month to month, with flexible budgets. You close deals on your normal eXp split, then decide whether to scale, hold steady, or pause your campaigns.

Many agents type “Brokerages with subscription services for lead access?” or “What are the commission structures for brokerages that provide agent leads?” because they have already felt the sting of a 50 or 60 percent split on “team leads.” Making It Rain flips that model. You pay for marketing, not for a permanent haircut on every check.

Here is the simple math. If you spend $250 to $500 per month and close even one reasonable transaction every few months, the ROI usually beats most paid lead programs that take a large cut at closing. You still keep your standard eXp commission split. There is no extra company split just because the lead came from Making It Rain. Independent real estate lead generation statistics for agents show many agents spend a few hundred dollars per month on paid leads, often with less structure than Making It Rain.

You can also scale up or down without asking permission. Have a busy season coming and want more buyer leads in a specific city or zip code. Increase the budget. Need to slow down during a life event or deep focus period. Pause or lower it. That flexibility is hard to find in traditional “lead packages.”

Agent Jill R. started with one accidental sale and treated real estate as a side project. After plugging into eXp’s systems, training, and lead tools, she went on to close 147 homes without holding listings. She credits the model and support for turning a small one off win into a full-time business.

Compared to handing over half your commission forever, a subscription you can turn off is the financial version of choosing a rental car instead of buying the whole dealership.

Are Making It Rain Leads Any Good? Follow Up, Quality, And Results

Making It Rain leads are as good as your follow up. Express Offers campaigns often attract very motivated sellers, while Home Search and FlexReach leads build a broad pipeline. The real edge comes from speed, empathy, and persistence. Most agents under call. The ones who win treat every lead as a conversation, not a quick sale.

When agents ask “How do brokerages vet and distribute leads to their agents?” they usually want to know if they are getting leftovers. With Making It Rain, you are not in a round robin. You receive leads from the campaigns you ordered. The key question becomes “What do you do in the first five minutes and the next thirty days?”

NAR data shows the average agent calls a lead about half the time it needs. Real contact often takes eight to twelve attempts across calls, texts, and emails. With Express Offers, these are often sellers under stress. Opening with “Tell me your story, what is going on?” builds trust. Sometimes the best fit is a retail sale, not a cash offer, yet that honest talk often wins the listing.

Armando G., a sponsor team member, built a system that closes more than twenty deals per month from Facebook ads. His story is simple. The ad gets the click, but the magic is in the conversation that follows. Consistent scripts and follow up turned paid leads into a predictable business.

If your current lead plan is “call once, blame the lead,” Making It Rain will work about as well as a treadmill used as a clothes rack. The power is in actually climbing on and pressing start.

What Agents Also Ask About Making It Rain

Is Making It Rain required to succeed at eXp Realty?
No. Making It Rain is optional. Many agents at eXp Realty build successful businesses using referrals, sphere, and organic lead sources. Making It Rain exists for agents who want paid online lead generation without managing ad platforms themselves, not as a requirement for production or advancement.

Who is Making It Rain best suited for?
Making It Rain works best for agents who already have a functioning CRM, automation, and consistent follow-up habits. Paid leads require fast response and structured nurture. Agents without established systems may struggle to convert any PPC leads effectively, regardless of the source.

How does Making It Rain compare to third-party PPC vendors?
Making It Rain centralizes PPC campaign management inside eXp Realty instead of outsourcing to outside vendors. Agents still fund advertising spend, but campaign setup, compliance, optimization, and CRM integration are handled internally, reducing complexity and minimizing the risk of policy or branding violations.

Does Making It Rain replace other eXp Realty lead programs?
No. Making It Rain supplements other eXp Realty lead programs such as Revenos and Zoocasa. Each program serves a different function within the overall lead ecosystem, allowing agents to layer paid advertising alongside referral-based or structured lead sources.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

Programs like Making It Rain are designed to support agent-funded lead generation through structured campaigns, but they do not operate in isolation or replace the broader eXp Realty experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is Making It Rain at eXp Realty?
Making It Rain is eXp Realty’s internal paid advertising program that allows agents to generate buyer and seller leads through brokerage-managed PPC campaigns. eXp oversees campaign setup, compliance, and delivery, while leads are routed directly into the agent’s CRM for follow-up and conversion.

How does pricing work for Making It Rain?
Making It Rain operates on a monthly subscription model in addition to the agent’s advertising spend. Agents are not charged referral fees at closing for these leads. Pricing structures, minimum budgets, and campaign options may vary and are subject to change based on program parameters.

Do Making It Rain leads affect commission splits or capping?
No. Transactions generated through Making It Rain close under the agent’s standard eXp commission split and count toward the annual cap in the same way as self-generated business. There are no additional referral deductions tied to these leads beyond normal brokerage terms.

Can Making It Rain campaigns be adjusted or paused?
Yes. Agents can generally adjust budgets, targeting locations, and campaign focus through the Making It Rain platform, subject to program guidelines and availability. Flexibility allows agents to scale spending up or down based on capacity, market conditions, or changes in business priorities.

Is Making It Rain available to new agents?
New agents may access Making It Rain after licensing and onboarding at eXp Realty. However, conversion success depends heavily on having a functioning CRM, prompt follow-up habits, and basic lead conversion training. Without systems in place, paid leads may not perform effectively.

  • Lead Generation Got Smarter: Meet eXp’s “My Link My Lead” Tool

  • All eXp Realty agents receive Canva Pro at no additional cost
  • Includes real estate–specific Canva templates and brand kits
  • Templates are preloaded with eXp branding and compliance standards
  • Agents can create flyers, social posts, presentations, and mailers
  • Canva Pro tools like Magic Write and background remover are included
  • Integrated into eXp Realty’s broader technology ecosystem

Most agents are told to “just market themselves” without being given real tools to do it efficiently. eXp Realty took a different approach by bundling Canva Pro free access directly into its technology platform, removing cost barriers and design friction for agents at every production level.

This article explains how Canva Pro free access fits into the broader eXp Realty Technology ecosystem available to eXp agents. Here’s your handy dandy index:

What Is eXp Canva?

Welcome to the glow-up your marketing has been waiting for. eXp Canva is the brand-new eXp marketing center built exclusively for eXp agents and, yes, it includes a Canva Pro free subscription. We’re talking full access to Canva Enterprise, aka the luxury suite of design tools, tailored specifically to meet eXp marketing guidelines (because “close enough” doesn’t cut it when you’re repping a top-tier online real estate brokerage).

The platform provides enterprise-level design functionality configured to align with eXp Realty branding and compliance standards.

Inside the New Marketing Center

This isn’t just a flashy rebrand—it’s a full marketing upgrade. The new eXp Canva center gives agents instant access to professional-grade designs for flyers, social posts, brochures, listing presentations, and even direct mail pieces.

You get an easy-to-use design flow, sleek Canva templates free for all eXp agents, and tools like a smart copywriting assistant and background remover that basically do the heavy lifting for you. In other words, you can now pass for a full marketing department… without actually having one.

From “Meh” to Magic: The Platform Shift

Let’s be honest: the old platform got the job done, but Canva takes things from “this will do” to “nails the polished look without the polished budget.” The rollout of enterprise-level Canva tools came with a treasure chest of upgrades: preloaded brand kits, the exclusive eXp Listings App, and support that actually supports you (no, really). The transition was smooth, and the impact? Immediate.

Why eXp Agents Love Canva

The real flex here? You get branded layouts for agents that are actually good. No more hacking together designs that look like a Craigslist ad. Instead, you get on-brand, ready-to-roll templates loaded with eXp Realty’s logos, fonts, and colors. You can tweak, personalize, and post with confidence—knowing your materials look as polished as your latest listing.

And with Canva’s advanced tools—like auto-resizing, smart editing suggestions, and a built-in content helper—you’re not just saving time. You’re working smarter, showing up better, and giving your brand a serious upgrade.

Features of eXp Canva (Including Canva Pro Free Tools) Let’s talk about the good stuff—the premium design tools that actually make your life easier. eXp Canva isn’t just some shiny new toy that looks good in a slideshow. It’s built specifically for eXp Realty agents who need pro-level marketing without spending pro-level hours (or cash).

Unlimited Access to Customizable Templates

Need a flyer yesterday? Want your listing presentation to stop looking like a PowerPoint from 2009? You’re in luck. eXp Canva gives agents unlimited access to free real estate Canva templates—already loaded with eXp branding (yes, including logos, fonts, and all those guidelines your broker keeps reminding you about).

From flyers and social media posts to postcards, property websites, and beyond, everything’s ready to plug, play, and post. It’s like having a marketing team in your pocket—minus the budget meetings.

Free Canva Pro Subscriptions for Every Agent

Let’s say it louder for the agents in the back: Canva Pro free access is available to all eXp agents. That means access to high-end tools like background remover, one-click resizing, and a built-in content generator.

You also get a massive stockpile of fonts, stock images, animations, and access to Canva real estate templates designed specifically for agents. Translation? You can finally ditch the generic clipart and create scroll-stopping content that won’t get lost in a feed full of filters and cat videos.

Create a Ton of Marketing Materials (Without the Meltdown)

Postcards? Check. Property websites? Yup. Direct mail pieces, infographics, listing presentations, and everything in between? Absolutely. Canva lets you make it all—fast. And with the eXp Listings App, you can drag and drop property info and photos straight into your designs without breaking a sweat.

Add in auto-resizing, real-time collaboration, and layout suggestions powered by AI, and suddenly marketing feels more like a creative playground than a second job.

How to Use eXp Canva (Without Wanting to Throw Your Laptop)

Here’s how to go from “new tool, what?” to marketing master in four easy steps:

Log in Like a Boss

Use your eXp email to access the eXp Canva platform. If you forget your password (we all do), hit “Forgot Password” or message eXp support—they’re surprisingly responsive. Bonus tip: two-factor authentication isn’t just for the paranoid. Use it.

Dive Into the eXp Template Library

Once inside, browse a huge collection of free Canva templates pre-approved by eXp’s branding team. Use filters to sort by category, goal, or listing type, and preview designs before customizing to avoid the dreaded “template regret.”

Customize with Style (and Speed)

This is where the fun happens. Drag and drop your property photos using the Listings App. Adjust colors, swap out text, and use tools like the built-in content generator to help with the copy. The background remover is perfect for cleaning up cluttered photos, and the resize tool helps your post go from Instagram square to email header in one click.

Download and Share Like a Pro

Once you’ve built your masterpiece, download it in whatever format works best—PDF, PNG, you name it. Want to share it? Post directly to social or send it to your team. Either way, you’ll pass for having a full-time designer… even if your “team” is just you and a latte.

Advantages of the New System

Moving to eXp Canva comes with a lot of benefits that easily elevate your campaigns. More importantly, it equips agents with effective tools and smart workflows designed for success. By integrating Canva Pro free tools, eXp Realty makes marketing more accessible, more efficient, and way less stressful—giving agents a professional edge without adding another line item to the budget.

Enhanced Customization Options for Agents

Canva gives you serious flexibility when it comes to design. Agents can fine-tune templates to match their personal branding, while pulling from Canva’s deep well of icons, graphics, and illustrations.

With features like Magic Resize, you can instantly reformat your content for multiple channels—perfect when you want to go from a postcard to an Instagram Story without starting from scratch.

This means faster turnaround, higher-quality output, and the freedom to experiment without falling off brand.

Streamlined Marketing Material Creation Process

The workflow inside eXp Canva is built for on-the-go agents. With its click-and-edit setup, agents can quickly produce social content, brochures, and more—without feeling like they’re stuck in a tech tutorial.

Prebuilt templates, smart suggestions, and real-time collaboration mean you can jump in, get it done, and move on to your next deal.

And the Canva Content Planner? It’s a total time-saver, letting you schedule social posts directly from the platform. No tab overload. No juggling apps.

Professional Branding Made Accessible

Branding doesn’t have to be a headache. With eXp Canva’s Brand Kit, agents can store their logos, fonts, and color palettes in one place, making it ridiculously easy to keep everything consistent and polished.

Premium visuals and high-quality real estate Canva templates make your marketing pop—whether it’s a listing flyer or a social campaign. It all works seamlessly with eXp marketing guidelines, so you can stay compliant without even thinking about it.

Broader Impacts on eXp Agents

Rolling out Canva Enterprise across the eXp ecosystem wasn’t just a software decision—it was a strategic shift. As more virtual real estate brokerages lean into tech-forward models, this move helps eXp agents do more, faster, with better results.

Because when your marketing looks sharp and takes minutes, not hours? You’re not just keeping up—you’re closing more deals.

Less Time Designing, More Time Closing

Outdated tools are out. Smart, nimble platforms are in. With customizable templates and smart features like AI-powered writing tools, agents are cutting content creation time—and seeing it pay off in real numbers.

Tech That Actually Moves the Needle

This isn’t about looking trendy. It’s about tech that works. With automation, built-in efficiency, and seamless integration into the eXp Realty ecosystem, this platform helps agents market like modern pros—no coding required.

Q4. Give agents the right tools, and they don’t just adapt—they outperform.

What Agents Also Ask About Canva Pro at eXp Realty

Is Canva Pro really free for all eXp Realty agents?

Yes. Canva Pro access is included for all active eXp Realty agents as part of the brokerage technology platform. Agents do not pay a separate subscription fee for Canva Pro features when using eXp’s marketing center.

How does eXp Canva differ from a standard Canva account?

eXp Canva is powered by Canva Enterprise and includes brokerage-approved templates, preloaded brand kits, and real estate–specific designs. Standard Canva accounts do not include eXp branding, compliance alignment, or the same curated template library.

Who benefits most from Canva Pro at eXp?

Agents who want to produce consistent marketing without hiring designers benefit most. Canva Pro is useful for solo agents, teams, and high-volume producers who need fast, repeatable content across listings, social media, and presentations.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty technology is designed to address agent use cases like marketing, branding, and visibility, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do all eXp Realty agents receive Canva Pro access?
Yes. All active eXp Realty agents receive Canva Pro access through the eXp Marketing Center at no additional cost. This includes premium design features, real estate templates, stock images, and brand tools that would otherwise require a paid Canva subscription outside the brokerage.

Can agents customize Canva templates with personal branding?
Yes. Agents can customize colors, fonts, images, layouts, and text while using eXp-approved Canva templates. Personal logos, headshots, and contact details can be added, allowing agents to maintain individual branding while still aligning with brokerage design and compliance standards.

Does eXp Canva replace hiring a professional designer?
For many agents, Canva Pro can replace the need for a designer for everyday marketing. Listing flyers, social posts, presentations, and mailers can be created in-house. Some agents may still hire designers for specialty branding projects, but routine marketing can typically be handled independently.

Is Canva Pro difficult to use for agents with no design background?
No. Canva is built specifically for non-designers. Its drag-and-drop interface, pre-sized templates, and guided layouts make it easy for agents to create professional-looking materials quickly, even without prior design experience or technical skills.

Are Canva designs compliant with eXp Realty branding standards?
Yes. Templates available inside eXp Canva are created to align with eXp Realty branding guidelines. This helps reduce the risk of off-brand marketing while still allowing agents flexibility to personalize designs for their market, listings, and individual business identity.

Ah, the traditional real estate office—the land of stale coffee, forced small talk, and printers that only jam when something’s urgent. Now enter eXp Workplace, the virtual real estate HQ where deals get done, agents stay sane, and nobody’s hoarding the good pens.

This isn’t just another digital dashboard with a fancy login screen. Nope. Workplace at eXp Realty is where team chats, training, networking, and memes about client insanity collide. Think of it like Facebook for eXp Realty, but valuable. You’ll find your exp directory here, talk shop in Workplace Chat, drop into EXPcon hype threads, and even DM top eXp executives, all the way up to founder, Glenn Stanford.

Whether you’re swapping leads at a regional rally or sharing wins in eXp events, this is your new digital home base. So, ready to ditch the water cooler gossip and level up your virtual real estate game? Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

What Is eXp Workplace?
Private, Secure, and Built for Speed
One Platform, Everyone You Need
Groups That Drive Real Results
Features of eXp Workplace
Messaging Without the Mayhem

What Is eXp Workplace?

Imagine if Facebook, Slack, and your brokerage had a baby—and it actually worked the way it’s supposed to. That’s eXp Workplace, the digital backbone of eXp Realty. It’s a private, connected communication platform built just for eXp agents and brokers.

This isn’t just a random group chat either. Workplace at eXp Realty is where real estate pros stay plugged in, share knowledge, and access everything from transaction tools to training sessions—all while wearing pajama pants. It’s become a core part of the eXp Realty agent experience, and frankly, it’s hard to imagine functioning without it.

Private, Secure, and Built for Speed

eXp Workplace chat is mobile-first (because yes, we’re all glued to our phones) and locked down with strong privacy features. You can talk referrals, listings, or client freakouts without worrying someone’s going to screen-grab it for the wrong reasons.

title: Final Walk Through Before Closing: What Agents Need to Know
excerpt: Key Points Final walk through confirms the home’s condition before closing Check that all agreed repairs are complete Ensure the seller removed all personal items…
content:
Key Points

  • Final walk through confirms the home’s condition before closing
  • Check that all agreed repairs are complete
  • Ensure the seller removed all personal items
  • Make sure the condition hasn’t changed
  • Document any damage or last-minute issues
  • Final walk through generally happens 24–48 hours before close
  • Skipping it risks costly surprises after closing

Ah, the final walk through before closing—where you get one more shot to discover the seller’s idea of “move-in ready” means “oops, we took the fridge and the closet doors.” Think it’s just a formality? Around 1 in 20 final walk throughs uncover issues serious enough to kill the deal.

Free Download: Final Walkthrough Checklist

Download our printable final walkthrough checklist – never miss a detail.

Download PDF

Whether it’s a light fixture swap-out that wasn’t in the deal or a surprise hole in the drywall, this isn’t the time to play it cool. A pre-closing walkthrough (or walk-through, or walkthrough—pick your preferred punctuation) is where buyers confirm that everything is still standing, functional, and as promised

So yeah, skipping it? Not recommended unless you enjoy filing complaints after you’ve already unpacked the moving truck. From who attends the final walk through to what to look for during it, we’re covering everything to help you nail this last step like the real estate pro you are. Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

What Is a Final Walk Through Before Closing?
Steps If You Find Issues During the Walkthrough

What Is a Final Walk Through Before Closing?

The final walk-through isn’t just a feel-good victory lap around the house. It’s your buyer’s last shot to make sure they’re not inheriting a leaky ceiling, a mysteriously absent stove, or “repairs” done with duct tape and hope. This is the moment to confirm that the property condition still matches the terms in the purchase agreement.

The goal? A clean and accurate property condition report, ideally without discovering a missing garage door or surprise pet damage. And if the home’s condition doesn’t match the contract, the buyer can hit the brakes faster than a bad credit score at underwriting.

Why the Final Walkthrough Actually Matters

This final check isn’t a chance to redecorate or get second thoughts on the paint color. It’s to confirm that repairs were done, nothing new is broken, and the seller didn’t swap your included appliances for something from 1992.

This is your buyer’s last shot at a property condition assessment before the keys change hands. If that fridge is gone or the AC stopped working—now’s the time to catch it. Not after closing, when it becomes your buyer’s expensive surprise.

When Does the Walkthrough Happen and Who Shows Up?

So when is the final walk through? Usually 24 to 48 hours before closing—right when everyone’s nerves are already fried. That gives just enough time to flag issues and maybe negotiate before the documents start flying.

Who attends the walkthrough? At a minimum the buyer and their real estate agent. Sometimes the seller tags along (usually awkwardly), and if the home’s older or had major repairs, bringing a home inspector can offer some extra peace of mind.

Depending on the size of the home, expect this step to take anywhere from a quick 15-minute breeze-through to a full hour-long real estate walkthrough. Pro tip: doing this in an empty house is much easier. You’ll spot damage, scuffs, and last-minute “oops” moments the seller left behind.

Setting the Right Expectations for the Buyer

Before stepping foot into the walkthrough, your buyer needs to know exactly what’s going down—and what’s definitely not. This isn’t the time to start spotting things they missed during the inspection and suddenly expect a new roof. The final walk through is a confirmation mission, not a second inspection with bonus drama.

Final Walkthrough vs. Home Inspection: Know the Difference

Let’s clear up the classic confusion: a home inspection happens early in the deal. That’s when buyers get the full diagnostic on the home—roof, foundation, outlets, ghost infestations, the works. Any problems found there? That’s when you negotiate repairs or concessions.

The final walk through, on the other hand, happens near the finish line. Think of it like a pre-delivery check. You’re there to make sure nothing’s changed since the deal was struck. It’s a verification step, not a redo of the inspection.

What the Final Walkthrough Is For:

Let’s keep it simple. The walkthrough before closing is for:
• Confirming agreed-upon repairs are actually done (no, sticky notes that say “FIX ME” don’t count)
• Ensuring the seller’s stuff is gone—not “mostly packed”
• Verifying everything that worked before still works now

What the Final Walkthrough Is Not For:

This is not your buyer’s chance to:
• Suddenly demand a full remodel
• Reopen negotiations because the wall color “hits different in daylight”
• Delay closing over stuff that should’ve been caught weeks ago

Make sure your buyer knows the rules of the final walkthrough checklist game. Because nothing kills a vibe—or a sale—like unrealistic expectations and last-minute finger-pointing. Clear expectations = smooth closing. You’re welcome.

Essential Final Walk Through Checklist (a.k.a. The “Let’s Not Regret This” List)

The final walk through before closing isn’t the time to wing it with good vibes and crossed fingers. Bring a real checklist and treat this like what it is—a last opportunity to make sure you’re not about to inherit someone else’s half-finished honey-do list. Snap pics of anything sketchy to back up your concerns and protect your buyer (and your commission).

Here’s what to check during your final walkthrough real estate adventure:

1. Verify Inspection Repairs

Remember all those inspection-related fixes the seller promised? Time to confirm they actually got done. Plumbing, HVAC, windows, stains from unknown origins—if it was flagged, it needs to be handled. Ask for receipts or contractor invoices. No proof = no peace of mind.

2. Ensure Personal Items Are Gone

Unless the contract says you’re inheriting the seller’s treadmill or ancient cat tree, the house should be empty. “Broom clean” does not mean “bonus boxes in the attic.” This step is part of the verification of property condition—your buyer should walk into a clean slate, not a leftover yard sale.

3. Examine the Interior – Any New Problems?

Do a full sweep for anything that wasn’t broken, dented, or mysteriously leaking the last time you saw the place. Sometimes the movers play fast and loose with doorways—and drywall. Check for:

  • New holes or scuffs
  • Damaged hardware
  • Doors or garage systems that no longer function

The goal of this final pre-closing check is to ensure the home didn’t take a beating on its way out the door. If something looks off, flag it now—because after closing, that repair bill becomes the buyer’s parting gift.

4. Examine Outdoor Areas

Don’t forget to walk the exterior. The final walk through of the house includes the backyard too. Fences suddenly falling? Moved staging pot uncovered a big hole in the deck? No one wants to discover a broken sprinkler system on move-in day—or worse, a pile of debris that didn’t make it into the moving truck.

Steps If You Find Issues During the Walkthrough

So, you’re 24 hours from closing, and the final check just revealed a fridge gone rogue or a couch-sized dent in the drywall. Now what? This is where speed and clarity matter.

Start with Clear Communication

Don’t panic—just document. Whip out your phone and start snapping photos of anything that doesn’t match the agreement. Whether it’s undone repairs, missing items, or new damage from the seller’s grand exit, you need evidence.

Then contact the listing agent right away. The faster you flag the problem, the faster the seller can fix it—or at least start groveling. Keep it factual. Notes and photos make sure everyone’s working from the same page.

Explore Options for Resolution

Depending on the issue, you’ve got a few paths forward:

  • Request Repairs: Quick fixes are possible if there’s still time—and the seller’s willing.
  • Ask for Credits or Concessions: Sometimes money off at closing is easier than scrambling to get someone in with a toolbox.
  • Bring in a Contractor: Get quotes for the damage to provide leverage for negotiations.
  • Cancel the contract: whether you can will be dependent on your agreement.

Evaluate the severity. A missing towel rack? Mildly annoying. A flooded basement? That’s gonna need more than a Home Depot run.

Delay Closing If Necessary

Major problems—like safety issues or incomplete repair work—might mean postponing the close. It’s not ideal, but better than taking ownership of a house that doesn’t meet contract terms. The goal here is to ensure the condition of the property aligns with what was promised.

Renegotiate Like a Pro

If the home’s condition doesn’t match what was agreed upon, renegotiating may be the cleanest fix. Adjust the price, ask for closing cost credits, or add a repair escrow to the deal. Go into the conversation with clear priorities—what’s a must, and what’s negotiable?

The ultimate goal? Get your buyer the home they expected without letting issues derail the entire deal. Handling this part like a boss shows you’re not just closing transactions—you’re protecting people. And in the world of final walk through real estate, that’s the difference between a frazzled agent and a trusted one.

Preparing for a Successful Final Walk Through Before Closing

The secret to a smooth walkthrough? Don’t show up empty-handed or wide-eyed. This isn’t the time for a casual stroll to “get a feel” for the place one last time. It’s your buyer’s final property condition check, and they need to walk through like they’re on a mission—because they are.

Coming in prepared helps catch missed repairs, abandoned junk, or any move-out surprises before they become your buyer’s responsibility. That’s where your trusty walkthrough checklist comes in—your personal “catch it now or regret it later” guide.

What to Bring for the Final Property Review

Heading into this last look? Pack smart. Here’s what you’ll want on hand during the pre-closing property review:

  • The purchase contract – to cross-check what was promised vs. what’s actually there
  • A notepad or mobile device – to record any “wait, what is that?” discoveries
  • A camera or phone – for photo evidence (you know, in case that fridge is mysteriously missing)
  • A flashlight – because basements and crawl spaces are never fully lit or fully innocent
  • Inspection report + receipts – to confirm every repair got done, not just “sort of handled”

Going into the final check before closing without these? That’s like showing up to a listing appointment without comps. Be thorough, be organized, and give your buyer peace of mind before they sign the dotted line.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a final walk through before closing?

A final walk through is the buyer’s last chance to ensure the home is in the agreed-upon condition before closing. It confirms repairs are complete, items are removed, and nothing new is broken.

When does the final walk through happen?

The final walk through usually happens 24 to 48 hours before closing. This gives time to resolve last-minute issues if anything’s out of place.

Who attends the final walk through?

Typically, the buyer and their real estate agent attend the final walk through. Sometimes the seller or an inspector may also be present, depending on the situation.

What should I look for during the final walk through?

During the final walk through, check that all repairs were done, personal items are removed, appliances and systems are working, and no new damage has occurred.

Can a seller refuse a final walk through?

While uncommon, a seller can technically refuse access. However, this raises red flags and may violate terms of the purchase agreement, depending on your contract and state laws.

Does the house need to be empty for the final walk through?

Yes, ideally. An empty house makes it easier to spot damage, check repairs, and ensure nothing was left behind. It also confirms the seller has vacated as agreed.

What happens if problems are found during the final walk through?

If issues are discovered, the buyer can request repairs, financial concessions, or delay closing. Documentation and quick communication with the listing agent are key.

title: Real Estate Jokes – Break the Ice, Build Connections
excerpt: Real estate can be stressful—but a well-placed joke can turn things around fast. Whether you’re dealing with indecisive buyers, tough negotiations, or that one client…
content:
Real estate can be stressful—but a well-placed joke can turn things around fast. Whether you’re dealing with indecisive buyers, tough negotiations, or that one client who sends 50 texts a day, humor is a game-changer. Ever had an awkward silence at a showing? A quick real estate pun can break the ice and keep the conversation flowing.

The right real estate jokes, one-liners, and puns can help you connect with clients, ease tense moments, and even add personality to your branding. Ready to inject some fun into your business? Here’s your handy dandy index:

Table of Contents

Understanding Real Estate Jokes
Practical Uses for Real Estate Jokes

Understanding Real Estate Jokes

Realtor jokes are special in their own way, often taking the hilarity from the mundane lives of real estate professionals. As with most real estate jokes, they’re the best kind of wit, based on sharp wordplay and puns, which makes them funny and illuminating. They’re widely accessible, making them perfect for real estate agents, buyers, sellers, or anyone with a basic understanding of the property market.

Their charm is in the universal appeal, making jokes about a struggle we all face, whether it’s that difficult customer or the always dreaded low commission. A perennial favorite has a buyer unable to make up his mind who says only “10% of people don’t like your carpet.” This hyperbole embodies the real fears of prospective homebuyers while eliciting some serious laughs. Much of the wit of these gags requires a second glance, rewarding the more thoughtful observers who linger on them.

What Makes Them Funny?

The best real estate jokes are based on universal themes. They play off the surprise of the market and focus on the weirdness of home showings. Wordplay and puns are the heart of their charm, needing only a little industry know-how to get the joke, not Ph.D. Level expertise.

Context is everything, as these are the kind of jokes that are most enjoyed by colleagues in the field who get the subtleties. Shared experiences amplify humor, as professionals can relate to scenarios like dealing with indecisive clients or the endless paperwork involved in transactions.

Humor in Real Estate Sales

Real estate humor can actually be a very valuable tool as a real estate sales professional, breaking the ice in client meetings or open houses and relieving tension. Second, it humanizes your brand, making it easier to build rapport with potential buyers and sellers, and creating more personable interactions.

Humorous asides can turn otherwise mundane real estate listings into page-turning thrillers. Finding that midpoint between professionalism and humor is absolutely key. The key is to keep jokes relatively tasteful and use them infrequently enough that they enhance, rather than distract from, the serious business at hand.

A light-hearted joke about how so many homes still have pink bathrooms will help you lower their guard. Additionally, it helps maintain the buzz on the sale.

Types of Real Estate Jokes

There are some real estate jokes that are just plain silly. From one-liners and puns to roasts, these jokes bring an interesting spark to conversations, leaving a lasting impression.

Here’s a closer look at the different types:

  • One-liners: Short and snappy, ideal for getting the conversation started.
  • Puns: Playful twists on words, often related to real estate terms.
  • Roasts: Gentle, humorous critiques that lighten the mood.

1. Agent-Specific Jokes

Agent-specific jokes target the unique challenges agents face every day. They feature relatable situations such as stressful open houses or high-maintenance clients, creating an inside joke that bonds realtors together at industry networking mixers.

Here are some common scenarios:

  • I’d say about ninety percent of the people in this city hate carpeting. The remaining 10% simply despise your carpeting.
  • How many agents to screw in a light bulb? One to be on top of the bulb, seven to change the bulb, and one to take the credit. One to charge you a 1.5% commission to break even.

2. Clever One-Liners

Quick quips are short but witty, and great for social media laughs. They nailed social media marketing and they really connect with clients.

Some memorable examples include:

  • How do real estate agents sell boats? They list them on Sail!
  • Why did the realtor go to the casino? Because she heard properties were high stakes!

3. Creative Puns

Creating real estate puns takes practice and a sharp sense of humor. Puns are an entertaining way to inject levity into otherwise serious topics, and they are super memorable in marketing collateral.

Examples include:

  • An ode to the many types of real estate humor, pun by pun.

4. Humorous Roasts

Roasting in real estate is when good-natured ribbing happens between industry friends. It builds camaraderie and brings levity to the workplace.

The key to success is understanding your audience. Examples of roasts include:

  • What’s the difference btw a realtor and a picnic table? The picnic table can support a family of four
  • What’s the difference between a Realtor and a Mortgage Broker? The Mortgage Broker knows he’s boring.

Engaging Real Estate Themes

Real estate themes such as open houses, mortgages, and house hunting provide a goldmine of comedic opportunities. Current trends, such as the rise of remote work, can inspire jokes about finding the perfect home office or Wi-Fi signal.

By integrating these themes into marketing strategies, whether humorously or sincerely, agencies can cut through the noise and find a relatable connection with clients. For example, you could run an entire campaign under a “house hunting” theme. That flirty method can lean into the light-hearted concept of looking for “the one.

  • Open houses
  • Mortgages
  • Property hunting
  • Home offices
  • Wi-Fi signals

When employed creatively, these themes can help to shine a humorous light on the often-stuffy world of real estate. If designed correctly, they can be embedded into promotional marketing materials to engage and immerse audiences and encourage a fun and engaging user journey.

Lighthearted and Fun Approaches

A sense of humor is key in real estate. Playful humor also allows realtors to engage with people on an emotional level. Through the creation of clever and caring content, you’re able to ease client worries.

Simple jokes like a realtor’s prayer that we may see a tripling of GCI this year can go a long way to help lighten the mood. Authenticity is key; make sure you deliver your humor in an authentic way and it will always land better.

  • Use puns related to real estate terms.
  • Share funny anecdotes from past experiences.
  • Create engaging social media posts with Canva.
  • Use humor to address common real estate concerns.

These strategies create a fun atmosphere while establishing trust and rapport.

Practical Uses for Real Estate Jokes

You may think that real estate jokes are a specialized hobby, but in fact they have very practical uses in the real estate business. Comedy can help defuse the atmosphere, relieve stress, break the ice, and ensure everyone feels comfortable and included.

When a client is comfortable, they are more likely to come forward with questions, allowing you to create the successful transactions they seek.

During open houses, jokes can create a more relaxed environment. In email newsletters, they can help you stay top-of-mind with prospects and clients. Real estate jokes provide excellent icebreakers at deal closings to relieve the tension.

In marketing campaigns, real estate humor draws potential clients in by creating memorable, engaging content. A well-placed, well-thought-out joke in an ad can leave a lasting impression.

It can dazzle as much as an awesome Facebook ad, one that pulls people in. Humor does come into the picture when it comes to customer service and follow-ups. A real estate agent might use a playful note to remind clients of their services, making interactions more personal and enjoyable.

Enhancing Client Interactions

Making humor a part of client communications will increase client satisfaction and loyalty. Strategies include:

  • Using humor in emails and newsletters
  • Sharing funny stories during meetings
  • Personalizing follow-ups with a humorous touch

When humor is involved, it becomes a memorable experience, but more importantly, it makes your clients feel special and understood. When clients connect your brand to those warm fuzzy feelings, they’ll come back and bring their friends.

Key Takeaways

  • Real estate jokes can help ease the tension of serious transactions, creating a fun shared experience for real estate pros and clients alike. They serve to break the ice when meeting clients and create a pleasant non-threatening appearance.
  • Knowing the running themes, puns, and background of real estate jokes can make them even more hilarious. Shared experiences between real estate professionals tend to make the humor hit even harder.
  • Each style of real estate joke, whether it’s a clever one-liner, a creative pun, or a humorous roast, has its own purpose. From marketing to networking, they provide an impactful forum to tackle these topics and more.

That’s a Wrap!

A well-timed real estate joke can break the ice, ease tension, and make you more memorable to clients and colleagues. Whether you’re using humor in marketing, networking, or daily interactions, the key is keeping it light, relevant, and well-placed. Make them laugh, build connections, and watch how it transforms your business!

Frequently Asked Questions

What makes real estate jokes popular?

The best real estate jokes hit home because they are a funny take on something familiar. They bring an enormous sense of levity, turning daunting subjects into digestible lessons. These real estate jokes are perfect for breaking the ice during serious meetings.

What are some common types of real estate jokes?

Popular varieties are puns, one-liners, and situational humor. Each style speaks to various audiences, from industry insiders to the layman. As with all good jokes, these can – and should!

Why incorporate humor in real estate education?

When it comes to teaching, humor is the great equalizer. It not only lightens the mood between dry technical specifications but creates an antidote to learner fatigue. This technique often produces greater understanding, deeper learning, and a more vibrant classroom atmosphere.

Are real estate jokes useful in marketing?

Short and funny real estate jokes are great conversation starters, they can boost engagement, and help you build a fun relationship with your audience. Beyond that, they’re a genius move to include in any real estate marketing strategy.

excerpt: Want to stay in the real estate game without juggling open houses, buyer texts at midnight, or pretending not to panic during inspections? Then, welcome…
content:
Want to stay in the real estate game without juggling open houses, buyer texts at midnight, or pretending not to panic during inspections? Then, welcome to the wonderful world of being a referral agent.

And if you’re with eXp Realty, it’s not just doable—it’s stupid simple.

With over 87,000 agents across 50 states and 27 countries, eXp gives you an insane referral reach. You don’t even need to leave your couch. Just use eXp’s slick, built-in referral tool to send a lead, sign a quick digital agreement, and lock in your cut when the deal closes. It seriously takes seconds.

Even better? eXp also has a full-blown Referral Division just for agents who only want to do referral business, meaning fewer fees and zero pressure to show properties or manage clients.

So if you’re ready to earn more without doing more, this blog will show you exactly how to build a referral business that pays off. Here’s your handy-dandy index:

Table of Contents

What is a Real Estate Referral?
Referral Example
eXp Referral Tool
Different From Other Brokerages
eXp Realty’s Referral Tool
How to Grow a Referral Business
There’s More
Frequently Asked Questions
What is a Real Estate Referral?

What is a Real Estate Referral?

First off, let’s break down what a real estate referral is. In the simplest terms, a real estate referral is when you, as an agent, connect a client with another agent who’s better suited to help them. Maybe the other agent has expertise in a specific area or they’re in a different location than you?  When you refer a client to this other agent and they successfully close a deal, you earn a referral fee—usually a percentage of the commission. So, the client gets the help they need and you get a nice reward for making the agent connection. It’s a win-win for everyone involved!

Referral Example

Let me give you an example to illustrate how this works in real life. Imagine Becky, a reputable eXp real estate agent in Los Angeles. Over the years, she’s built a strong network and frequently gets inquiries from clients who are outside her service area. Becky also has a YouTube channel where she lets people know that she can help them find a realtor in all 50 states and in 24 countries.

One day, Becky gets an email from Adam, a potential client who’s looking to relocate to New York City and he wants to find an agent to help him buy a home there. Becky isn’t located in New York City but she can locate an eXp New York City agent to help Adam.

Let’s say Becky locates Mike, an eXp agent who’s a specialist in New York City. Becky reaches out to Mike, and fills him in on Adam’s situation, and Mike agrees to take on the referral in exchange for a 25% referral fee. Becky and Mike sign a referral agreement and Mike starts working with Adam. After a few weeks, Mike successfully helps Adam find the perfect home, negotiates the deal, and closes the sale.

Mike earns his commission, and in line with the agreement, Becky receives 25% of that commission as her referral fee. Not only is Adam thrilled with his new home, but Becky also enhances her reputation by connecting her client with a top-notch agent.

eXp Referral Tool

Now let’s show how easy and fast it is for eXp referral agents to make referrals using eXp Realty’s Referral Tool. The whole thing is so simple!  You have a client with a need, you find an agent, make a deal, and get paid when the deal happens.  Let’s break down those steps – everything happens in just seconds with eXp’s Referral Tool!

Finding an Agent

There are three ways to find an agent for your client:

  1. Perhaps you already know one,
  2. Perhaps someone in your sponsor line knows one, or
  3. You can search in eXp Realty’s referral tool which already connects to the eXp Agent Directory.

And, don’t forget here, that eXp Realty has over 87,000 agents around the globe so finding an eXp agent is usually pretty easy! 

  • Just hit “Select Agent” and you’ll find an agent in no time.
  • When you find an agent, call them, fill them in on the client’s needs, and agree to your referral percentage commission for a closed deal.
  • Fill out the rest of the form on the referral tool, sign the agreement and exp takes care of the rest!

Different From Other Brokerages

If you’re a referral agent at another brokerage, you know this process is either not possible because you can’t provide referrals outside your location or it involves tons of time. You need to find an agent, the hard way using the internet or, if you’re lucky, a company agent directory and then learn about the agent.  After that’s done, there’s lots more time expended. 

Like endless time to go back and forth with the agent, track down the right forms, get all the correct parties to sign the forms, collect the forms, submit those to the right place, and then follow up with the agent to see if any deal was closed and make sure you get paid what was agreed to.

eXp Realty’s Referral Tool

But with eXp Realty’s official referral tool, everything is streamlined. First, eXp’s referral tool provides an official eXp referral form that’s compliant across all U.S. states and territories. You literally have nothing to worry about or waste time thinking about.

You can easily find an agent and auto-populate the form with the receiving agent’s name and contact information.  Then you enter the other necessary details and sign the form.

Now eXp takes care of the rest.  The tool sends the agreement to all parties for eSignatures including the appropriate brokers, while shielding your client’s information until the agreement is fully signed. This ensures your client’s privacy is protected, and you’ve got a legally binding agreement in place.

After the agreement is signed, the client’s information is automatically shared with the receiving agent, and all you have to do is add your referral agreement to skyslope, sit back and wait for your referral fee to come in. eXp keeps track of it all! It’s so simple!

How to Grow a Referral Business

Now that you know how easy it is to make referrals and how lucrative it can be, let’s talk about how you can get started making referrals.  Here are some steps:

Step 1: Focus on the Referral Agent Business

This is an awesome opportunity to grow your real estate career and boost your reputation, and the best part? You don’t need extra licenses or courses to jump in. Once you’ve got your real estate license and you’re aligned with a brokerage, you can immediately start positioning yourself as a reliable referral agent. If you’re good at generating leads, this is where you can really shine.

Step 2: Build a Strong Referral Network

The quality of your referrals depends on the agents in your network, so build it wisely. Create a list of trusted agents with a proven track record of closing deals and delivering excellent service. Don’t just refer clients to friends—your reputation is at stake. Make sure the agents you’re referring to will get the job done. Keep your network organized in a spreadsheet or CRM so you can track your contacts easily.

Step 3: Find Prospects Needing Service Outside Your Area

Stay alert for opportunities when people mention relocating or needing real estate services you don’t provide. By being proactive, you can create a steady stream of referrals from those needing service outside your location or specialty.

And if you’re with eXp, you’ve got a bonus: eXp’s My Link My Lead platform. It helps you identify people in your sphere who are moving, investing, or looking for properties outside your area. It’s a game-changer for keeping tabs on potential referrals. We’ll break down that awesome platform in a future blog.

Step 4: Promote Your Referral Network

If you want to grow your referral business, you’ve got to make sure people know about it. Set aside time for events, conferences, and networking to expand your reach. Add your referral agent services to your website, business cards, social media profiles, and marketing materials.

Remind your clients—whether through newsletters or social media—that you can assist with transactions in other locations or even with different property types, like commercial real estate. Keep yourself at the top of their minds as their go-to referral resource.

Step 5: Use the Referral Tool

You’ve worked to find people who need real estate services that you can’t provide. Now just hit the link to the eXp Referral Tool and spend a few seconds to get that cash ball rolling!

There’s More

But here’s where it gets even better: eXp Realty has an actual Referral Division designed for agents who want to focus only on referral income. No MLS fees, no local board dues, no stress. You stay licensed with eXp, keep your branding, and send referrals through the official system just like every other agent—but without the day-to-day hustle of listings and buyers.

That means:

  • You can keep your license active and productive without running a traditional business
  • You still earn commission splits (typically 25%–35%) from closed deals you referred
  • You skip the typical overhead and transaction responsibilities full-service agents take on

Whether you’re semi-retired, pivoting careers, managing a side hustle, or just want to monetize your network, the eXp Referral Division is built for exactly that.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a Real Estate Referral?

A real estate referral is when an agent connects a client with another agent who’s better suited to help the client. The referring agent earns an agreed-upon commission when the agent working with the client successfully closes a deal.

What is an example of an agent referral?

Imagine Becky, an agent in Los Angeles, gets an email from Adam, a potential client who’s looking to relocate to New York City. Becky locates Mike, a New York City agent to refer the client to. Mike successfully helps the client the perfect home, negotiates the deal, and closes the sale. Becky receives the agreed-upon commission as the referral agent.

What is eXp’s referral tool?

eXp Realty’s Referral Tool is an online tool for eXp agents where they can find eXp agents in 50 states and 24 countries to refer their clients to for their non-local needs. The tool is fast for agents to use. After filling in client details and locating an agent, eXp Realty does everything else. They get the correct signatures and make sure the referral agent gets paid upon a successful closing.

Here’s what BoldTrail’s IDX real estate website brings to the table:

Smart Property Search That Feels Like Magic

BoldTrail includes a powerful MLS search engine with filters so specific, clients can search for homes with a dog shower, wine fridge, and three-car garage. Think: interactive maps, saved searches, and polygon draw tools—all fully mobile-ready so buyers can shop from their phones in line at Starbucks.

Lead Capture That Doesn’t Scare People Off

Forget clunky pop-ups and “Sign up now!” desperation vibes. BoldTrail’s real estate agent website with IDX uses built-in lead capture forms that flow naturally with the search experience. CTAs like “Request a Showing” or “Save This Home” pop up just when users are most interested—aka, when they’re emotionally attached and ready to click.

Automated Market Reports (So You Look Like a Genius)

Your IDX website automatically sends market reports to keep clients in the loop without you lifting a finger. Want to be seen as the local expert? These reports deliver price trends, active inventory, and time-on-market stats straight to their inbox like clockwork.

Home Valuation Tools for Nosy Neighbors and Future Sellers

BoldTrail’s built-in home valuation tools are click magnets. Homeowners type in an address to see what their place is worth, you collect a lead, and boom—now you’ve got someone to follow up with when they realize Zillow’s “Zestimate” is garbage.

Behavior Tracking That Makes You Psychic (Sort Of)

Yes, BoldTrail watches what users are clicking on. It tells you who’s looking, how often, and what they’re drooling over. That way, you know who’s serious and who’s just “browsing for fun.” Use it to prioritize your follow-up and stop chasing ghosts.

Text + Email Drip Campaigns Built Right In

When someone signs up through your IDX MLS website, the system can automatically trigger a drip campaign with helpful content, follow-ups, and reminders. Basically, it nurtures leads while you sleep or binge that Netflix show you’re “totally behind on.”

Mobile-Responsive, SEO-Ready, and Customizable

BoldTrail sites are designed for both humans and Google. They look good on every device, load fast, and come with baked-in SEO features to help you rank for local search terms like “homes for sale in [your town that isn’t boring].”

What Agents Also Ask About IDX Websites

Is an IDX website required to succeed as a real estate agent?

An IDX website is not legally required, but it has become a practical necessity. Buyers expect searchable listings, saved searches, and alerts. Without IDX, agents rely on third-party portals, which limits branding, data ownership, and long-term lead generation.

How does an IDX website differ from Zillow or Redfin?

Zillow and Redfin are consumer portals that monetize agent traffic. An IDX website keeps searches on the agent’s own platform, allowing direct lead capture, branded experiences, and automated follow-up without competing ads or lead reselling.

Who benefits most from having an IDX website?

Agents focused on online visibility, organic SEO, and long-term lead pipelines benefit most. IDX websites are especially valuable for agents building local authority, nurturing buyers over time, and creating repeatable digital lead systems.

Why This Matters Before You Join eXp Realty

eXp Realty technology is designed to address agent use cases like lead capture, marketing automation, and compliance, but it does not operate in isolation or replace the broader brokerage experience.

At eXp Realty, all agents receive the same core brokerage platform, including compliance, compensation, and access to company divisions. What differs is the sponsor ecosystem an agent aligns with.

The sponsor is selected during the application process, before most agents have used the brokerage’s systems, explored its tools, or seen how sponsorship works in real life. Knowing where sponsorship fits within eXp Realty’s overall structure helps agents view this decision in the right context.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do all eXp Realty agents receive an IDX website?

No. IDX websites are provided only to agents who select a CRM that includes website functionality, such as BoldTrail or Lofty. Agents who choose Cloze do not receive an IDX website through eXp and must obtain one independently if needed.

Is the IDX website at eXp Realty really free?

Yes. The IDX website is included as part of eXp Realty’s $85 monthly technology fee when using BoldTrail or Lofty. There are no separate IDX licensing fees, hosting charges, or required website contracts through the brokerage.

Can agents customize their eXp IDX website?

Yes. Agents can customize branding, colors, images, widgets, pages, blogs, and local content. While templates provide structure, the website can be tailored to reflect an agent’s personal brand and market focus.

Does an IDX website replace CRM or transaction management tools?

No. The IDX website is connected to the CRM for lead capture and automation, but transaction management and compliance are handled separately through eXp’s centralized systems. Each component serves a distinct role within the platform.
